_meta.editorial_owner: Thomas W. Hilgers, MD; FertilityCare Centers of America

evidence_tier_definitions.peer_reviewed: Published in a peer-reviewed journal, verified PMID/DOI.
evidence_tier_definitions.clinic_presentation: Clinic document, slide deck, or non-peer-reviewed manuscript. Usable with caveat.
evidence_tier_definitions.clinical_experience: Clinician-attributed statement without a traceable document. Cite with explicit framing.
evidence_tier_definitions.organizational_primary: FEMM/RHRI, Saint Paul VI, clinic fact sheet, or NeoFertility institutional document (not peer-reviewed article, not clinician-attributed). Cite with document name and date.

facts.claim: After stopping birth control pills, couples should avoid achieving pregnancy during the first 3-6 cycles due to possible increased frequency of spontaneous abortion.

source.title: Chapter 10: Special Instructions and Applications
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Avoid achieving pregnancy during first 3-6 cycles after stopping pill due to possible increased frequency of spontaneous abortion" Section: Instructions for Coming Off Birth Control Pills.

facts.claim: Seminal fluid may be discharged following intercourse for up to 72 hours and frequently has the visual and sensual characteristics of Peak-type mucus.

source.title: Chapter 10: Special Instructions and Applications
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Seminal fluid will be discharged following intercourse and may continue for up to 72 hours." Section: Seminal Fluid Instruction.

facts.claim: The Seminal Fluid Instruction (SFI) requires urinating within one hour after intercourse, performing 3-5 Kegel's exercises while bearing down in alternating fashion, and wiping until seminal fluid is gone.

source.title: Chapter 10: Special Instructions and Applications
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Urinate after intercourse (within one hour); Bear down and do several (3 to 5) Kegel's exercises in an alternating fashion" Section: The Seminal Fluid Instruction (SFI).

facts.claim: A normal cycle is defined as one in which the woman has confidently identified the Peak Day AND usual menstrual flow occurred 8 to 16 days later.

source.title: Chapter 10: Special Instructions and Applications
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "A cycle in which the woman has confidently identified the Peak Day AND usual menstrual flow occurred 8 to 16 days later." Section: A Note About Normal Cycles.

facts.claim: Infertility couples using the SFI during fertile time should wait thirty minutes prior to employing the instruction to allow sperm to migrate to the fallopian tubes.

source.title: Chapter 10: Special Instructions and Applications
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the couple with an infertility problem should be specifically instructed to wait thirty minutes prior to employing the instruction" Section: SFI for Infertility Couples.

facts.claim: It typically requires three to five acts of intercourse with successful employment of the SFI to recommend advancement of system instructions.

source.title: Chapter 10: Special Instructions and Applications
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "it requires three to five acts of intercourse with the successful employment of the SFI to recommend advancement of the system instructions" Section: Advancing Instructions with the SFI.

facts.claim: Arousal fluid comes from the Bartholin's glands located at the back of the vaginal opening, tends to dissipate quickly and disappear by an hour following stimulation.

source.title: Chapter 10: Special Instructions and Applications
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Arousal fluid is a lubricative fluid that comes from the Bartholin's glands, which are located in the back portion of the opening of the vagina." Section: Arousal Fluid Instruction.

facts.claim: A double Peak is defined as two Peak-type mucus buildups in the same cycle with a gap greater than 4 days between the first Peak Day and resumption of Peak-type mucus.

source.title: Chapter 10: Special Instructions and Applications
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "there is a gap greater than 4 days between the appearance of the first Peak Day and the resumption of Peak-type mucus discharge" Section: Double Peak.

facts.claim: A split Peak is defined as a gap no greater than 4 days between observations of Peak-type mucus; Peak-type mucus days are separated, generally by dry days.

source.title: Chapter 10: Special Instructions and Applications
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "A 'split' Peak can be defined as the occurrence of a gap no greater than 4 days between observations of Peak-type mucus." Section: Split Peak.

facts.claim: Long menstrual cycles are defined as cycles consistently longer than 38 days in duration for classification purposes.

source.title: Chapter 10: Special Instructions and Applications
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Long menstrual cycles can generally be defined as cycles that are consistently longer than 38 days in duration (for classification purposes)." Section: Long Cycles.

facts.claim: Post-Peak infertility using yellow stamps always begins at the end of the fourth day post-Peak.

source.title: Chapter 10: Special Instructions and Applications
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Infertility always begins at end of fourth day post-Peak" Section: Special Instructions: Post-Peak, Non-Peak Mucus.

facts.claim: Total breastfeeding can continue for the first 6 months; after 6 months, supplementation becomes necessary.

source.title: Chapter 10: Special Instructions and Applications
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Total breastfeeding can continue for the first 6 months; after 6 months, supplementation becomes necessary." Section: Breast Feeding.

facts.claim: Premenstrual spotting is defined as 3 or more days of light, very light, or brown bleeding preceding moderate or heavy flow; 1-2 days of spotting are considered part of next menstrual period.

source.title: Chapter 10: Special Instructions and Applications
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Premenstrual spotting means 3 or more days of light, very light, or brown bleeding that precedes the beginning of a moderate or heavy flow." Section: Premenstrual Spotting.

facts.claim: Premenstrual spotting should be charted as fertile for 3 cycles; after 3 cycles, continue charting bleeding but follow basic instructions based on presence or absence of mucus.

source.title: Chapter 10: Special Instructions and Applications
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Chart as fertile for 3 cycles" Section: Special Instructions for Premenstrual Spotting.

facts.claim: Infertility couples should avoid all genital contact during the first complete menstrual cycle to develop confidence in mucus identification and evaluate the mucus cycle.

source.title: Chapter 10: Special Instructions and Applications
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Avoid all genital contact during the first complete menstrual cycle to develop confidence in mucus identification" Section: Teaching Considerations for Infertility.

facts.claim: For totally breastfeeding women, the first 56 days (8 weeks) after birth are considered infertile regardless of mucus pattern.

source.title: Chapter 10: Special Instructions and Applications
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Consider first 56 days after birth infertile" Section: If TOTALLY Breastfeeding - First 8 Weeks Infertile Period.

facts.claim: For partially breastfeeding women, the first 8 weeks after birth are NOT considered infertile.

source.title: Chapter 10: Special Instructions and Applications
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "No Automatic Infertile Period: First 8 weeks after birth date are NOT considered infertile" Section: If PARTIALLY Breastfeeding.

facts.claim: Post-abortion charting should begin when bleeding decreases (usually within 1 week following the incident), with genital contact avoided for first 4 weeks of charting.

source.title: Chapter 10: Special Instructions and Applications
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Begin charting when bleeding decreases (usually within 1 week following the incident)" Section: Special Instructions: Post-Abortion.

facts.claim: The CrMS distinguishes Adolescent (not genitally active) as 17 years of age and under, and Single Woman (not genitally active) as 18 years of age and older.

source.title: Chapter 10: Special Instructions and Applications
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Adolescent (Not Genitally Active): 17 years of age and under" Section: Category Distinctions.

facts.claim: The CREIGHTON MODEL System (CrMS) is a standardized modification of the Billings Ovulation Method.

source.title: Chapter 12: Decision Making in the CrMS
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the CREIGHTON MODEL System (CrMS) (a standardized modification of the Billings System)" Section: Introduction.

facts.claim: Per the CrMS 'Trinitarian Proposal' (1+1+1=1), couples using fertile days to achieve pregnancy co-create with God a new, eternal human life.

source.title: Chapter 12: Decision Making in the CrMS
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "they have the opportunity to co-create with God a new, eternal human life." Section: The Sacramental Moment of Human Procreation.

facts.claim: The CrMS places equal emphasis on both achieving and avoiding pregnancy, enabling a per-cycle decision on whether pregnancy should be attempted.

source.title: Chapter 12: Decision Making in the CrMS
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "With the CrMS, the emphasis is equally upon both achieving and avoiding pregnancy." Section: The CrMS Approach: True Family Planning.

facts.claim: In the CrMS, users know on any given day whether they are fertile or infertile, and using days of fertility adopts the system as a means of achieving pregnancy.

source.title: Chapter 12: Decision Making in the CrMS
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Using days of fertility abandons the system as a means of avoiding pregnancy and adopts the system as a means of achieving pregnancy" Section: Teaching Principles.

facts.claim: CrMS teaching requires instructors to present fertility and infertility principles without contraceptive bias.

source.title: Chapter 12: Decision Making in the CrMS
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the teacher must teach the basic principles of fertility and infertility and not be contraceptively biased." Section: Teaching Principles.

facts.claim: The decision to use CrMS to achieve or avoid pregnancy is framed as a joint decision between husband and wife, not the provider.

source.title: Chapter 12: Decision Making in the CrMS
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "This decision, to achieve or avoid pregnancy, should be a joint one between husband and wife." Section: Freedom of Use.

facts.claim: The CrMS decision-making framework is termed the Principle of Selective Intercourse, or Mutually Selective Intercourse (MSI) when emphasizing joint spousal choice.

source.title: Chapter 12: Decision Making in the CrMS
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Since the choices should be mutual, the term mutually selective intercourse (MSI) applies." Section: Decision Making.

facts.claim: Hilgers argues periodic abstinence is not specific to natural methods because contraceptive users also abstain between sexual contacts.

source.title: Chapter 12: Decision Making in the CrMS
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Those who use contraceptives also practice periodic abstinence since they do abstain from genital intercourse between one sexual contact and the next." Section: Why 'Periodic Abstinence' is NOT Specific to Natural Methods.

facts.claim: The CREIGHTON MODEL System (CrMS) can be used to either achieve or avoid pregnancy, a use-related concept unique to natural methods of fertility regulation.

facts.claim: The periovulatory estradiol (E2) profile is obtained by drawing E2 levels every other day starting from Peak-5 (or the first day of post-peak mucus per the Creighton Model).

facts.claim: In 18 consecutive patients with a 'Frequent 2W' (wet without lubrication) charting pattern, 15 (83.3%) had positive cervical cultures indicating low-grade cervical infection.

source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "In 18 consecutive patients where the cervix has been cultured... 15 of them had positive cultures (83.3%)" Section: Frequent 2W Observations.

facts.claim: The point of change (POC) from an ESP marks the beginning of fertility and correlates with rising estradiol-17β, marking the start of the follicular phase.

source.title: Chapter 25: Chronic Discharges and CrMS
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The POC correlates with the rising levels of estradiol (17β) and is the beginning of the follicular phase" Section: Overview.

facts.claim: Of 4 patients with persistent greenish CrMS discharge who were cultured, 3 had positive polymicrobial cultures including Gardnerella, Prevotella, and enterococcus species.

source.title: Chapter 25: Chronic Discharges and CrMS
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Included enterococcus faecalis, Gardnerella vaginalis, Prevotella divia, Prevotella oralis, Propionibacterium acnes and Alpha-hemolytic pneumococci." Section: Green Discharge.

facts.claim: Implantation mucus is a Peak-type discharge observed after Peak Day in early pregnancy; it is a normal variant likely of endometrial origin and requires no treatment.

source.title: Chapter 25: Chronic Discharges and CrMS
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "This pattern, which has been observed in a number of patients in early pregnancy, is called 'implantation mucus.'" Section: Implantation Mucus.

facts.claim: In the K+3 study of 26 patients across 47 cycles, 100% of ovulations occurred by K+3, confirmed by progesterone >2.3 ng/mL or ultrasound follicular rupture.

source.title: Chapter 25: Chronic Discharges and CrMS
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "In all 47 cycles (100%) ovulation occurred by K+3 as indicated by a progesterone level >2.3 ng/mL or follicular rupture demonstrated by U/S." Section: K+3 Study.

facts.claim: In the K+3 study, mean K+3 progesterone was 11.8 ng/mL (range 4.1–28.5) in 35 cycles; ultrasound-confirmed ovulations occurred between K-2 and K+1.

source.title: Chapter 25: Chronic Discharges and CrMS
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The mean progesterone level for the K+3 group (n=35) was 11.8 ng/mL with a range of 4.1-28.5 ng/mL." Section: Study Results.

facts.claim: The K+3 study population (n=26) had mean age 31.8 years (range 22–40), average gravidity 1.5, and average parity 1.3.

source.title: Chapter 25: Chronic Discharges and CrMS
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The age of this population averaged 31.8 years, but ranged from 22 through 40 years of age. The average gravidity was 1.5 and parity 1.3" Section: Continuous Peak-type Mucus.

facts.claim: A continuous pasty, cloudy (PC) discharge in CrMS is most likely a normal variant and does not constitute a criterion for cervical eversion.

source.title: Chapter 25: Chronic Discharges and CrMS
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "A continuous pasty, cloudy discharge (PC) is most likely a normal variant." Section: Basic Discharge Pattern Types.

facts.claim: CrMS criteria for cervical eversion include sticky (1/4"), tacky (1/2–3/4"), or gummy discharge during early pre-Peak phase (before the 6th day pre-Peak).

source.title: Chapter 25: Chronic Discharges and CrMS
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "A sticky (1/4 inch), tacky (1/2-3/4 inch) or gummy discharge present during the early pre-Peak phase of the cycle" Section: Criteria for Identifying Cervical Inflammation.

facts.claim: CrMS criteria for cervical eversion also include any sticky, tacky, stretchy (≥1 inch), or gummy discharge seen from the fourth post-Peak day onward.

source.title: Chapter 25: Chronic Discharges and CrMS
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Any sticky, tacky, stretchy (1 inch or more) or gummy discharge seen any time from the fourth day post-Peak." Section: Criteria for Identifying Cervical Inflammation.

facts.claim: Pasty cloudy discharge, yellow discoloration of mucus, and premenstrual mucus are explicitly NOT criteria for cervical eversion in CrMS.

source.title: Chapter 25: Chronic Discharges and CrMS
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "A pasty, cloudy discharge is not a criteria. Yellow discoloration of the mucus is not a criteria. Premenstrual mucus is not a criteria." Section: Criteria.

facts.claim: Progesterone stimulates production of Type G mucus in the cervix, which acts as a barrier to sperm penetration (closure of the biological valve).

source.title: Chapter 26: Isomolecular Hormones vs Heteromolecular Artimones
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Progesterone inhibits the effects of estrogen on the endocervical mucus-producing crypts and stimulates the production of Type G mucus" Section: Effects of Progesterone and Estrogen.

facts.claim: Estrogen stimulates endocervical crypts to produce Type E mucus, essential for sperm migration and penetration through the cervix (opening the biologic valve).

source.title: Chapter 26: Isomolecular Hormones vs Heteromolecular Artimones
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "It will stimulate the endocervical crypts to produce Type E mucus, which is essential for sperm migration and penetration" Section: Effects of Progesterone and Estrogen.

facts.claim: Progesterone is thermogenic, which accounts for the elevated basal body temperature in women during the postovulatory phase of the cycle.

source.title: Chapter 26: Isomolecular Hormones vs Heteromolecular Artimones
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The progesterone hormone is also thermogenic, which accounts for the elevated basal body temperature in women during the postovulatory phase" Section: Effects of Progesterone and Estrogen.

facts.claim: Ovulation occurs on day 14 of the cycle in only 13.5 percent of cycles, making calendar-based timing unreliable for hormone supplementation.

source.title: Chapter 27: Cooperative Progesterone and Estrogen Replacement
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "ovulation occurs on day 14 of the cycle in only 13.5 percent of cycles." Section: Overview.

facts.claim: The Peak Day reliably estimates the day of ovulation, with ovulation occurring on Peak Day +/- 2 days in 95.4 percent of cycles.

source.title: Chapter 27: Cooperative Progesterone and Estrogen Replacement
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "ovulation occurring on Peak Day +/- 2 days in 95.4 percent of cycles." Section: Overview.

facts.claim: The ovulation sequence completes by Peak +2 in virtually 100 percent of patients, and the average length of the post-Peak phase is 12 days.

source.title: Chapter 27: Cooperative Progesterone and Estrogen Replacement
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The ovulation sequence is over in virtually 100 percent of patients by Peak +2 and the average length of the post-Peak phase of the cycle is 12 days." Section: Oral Micronized Progesterone Capsules.

facts.claim: In Figure 28-3, a patient initiating a 45-minute jazzercise program with sit-ups and leg lifts plus a weight reduction diet (losing 10 pounds, from 122 to 112 lbs) developed completely dry cycles across three consecutive cycles.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "45-minute jazzercise program with sit-ups and leg lifts...weight reduction diet and lost 10 pounds (from 122 lbs. to 112 lbs.)" Section: Figure 28-3.

facts.claim: In the Figure 28-3 case, when strenuous exercise and dieting were discontinued, normal mucus cycles returned and the patient subsequently achieved pregnancy resulting in a live-born baby boy.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "When she did this, she reverted back to having normal mucus cycles. A few cycles later, she became pregnant" Section: Figure 28-3.

facts.claim: In a wedding-stress case (Figure 28-4), the first cycle (37 days) showed a classic double Peak with first Peak on day 12 and a second mucus buildup days 18-26.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "a 37-day cycle...Peak Day on day 12 and then a second mucus buildup beginning on day 18 and concluding on day 26." Section: Figure 28-4.

facts.claim: In Figure 28-4 second cycle (40 days), prolonged mucus buildup concluded on day 32 with a short 8-day post-Peak phase, suggesting the entire cycle was hormonally abnormal.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "40-day cycle...prolonged mucus buildup that concludes on day 32...very short post-Peak phase numbering only eight days" Section: Figure 28-4.

facts.claim: Stress-induced cycle aberrations must be differentiated from cervical inflammation, but stressed cycles do not meet the diagnostic criteria for cervical inflammation (see Chapter 25).
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Neither of these two cycles meets the criteria for a cervical inflammation (see Chapter 25)." Section: Figure 28-4.

facts.claim: The 'double' Peak phenomenon from stress may represent a protective mechanism that delays ovulation until the stressor is relieved.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "It is a situation that can happen to any woman; it may be a protective mechanism at work." Section: Overview.

facts.claim: Strenuous exercise combined with weight loss (dieting) can convert normal mucus cycles into infertile dry cycles, representing a stress-induced functional infertility pattern.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Because of the dry cycle pattern, she converted her cycles to infertile cycles." Section: Figure 28-3.

facts.claim: Charting with the CREIGHTON MODEL FertilityCare System (CrMS) provides women with self-knowledge that enables lifestyle evaluation to identify and reduce stress-related cycle effects.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "With the self-knowledge that comes with charting the CREIGHTON MODEL FertilityCare™ System (CrMS), women may be able to take a look at their lifestyle" Section: Self-Knowledge.

facts.claim: A classic charting aberration due to stress is the 'double' Peak, where stress delays ovulation and a second Peak occurs after the stress is relieved. Ovulation occurs with the second Peak.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The classic example a charting aberration due to stress is the 'double' Peak. This is a situation in which stress delays ovulation" Section: Overview.

facts.claim: Stress-induced charting effects include prolonged Peak-type mucus buildup, delayed Peak Day and ovulation, conversion to completely dry cycles, or conversion to limited mucus cycles.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "prolonged Peak-type mucus buildup while others may have a delay in the Peak Day and ovulation. In others, the charting pattern may turn totally dry" Section: Overview.

facts.claim: In a case example (Figure 28-1), a minor surgical procedure on day 11 delayed the Peak Day to day 20 (9 days after surgery), extending cycle length to 34 days.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "minor surgical procedure on day 11 of her third cycle of charting has a Peak Day occurring on day 20 of the cycle or nine days after the surgery" Section: Case Example Figure 28-1.

facts.claim: In a bereavement case (Figure 28-2), a grandfather's death on day 15 and funeral on day 22 delayed Peak Day to day 27, with a 20-day mucus cycle and 42-day overall cycle length.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "her grandfather died (on day 15)...funeral was delayed until day 22...Peak Day did not occur until day 27" Section: Figure 28-2.

facts.claim: Following the bereavement case in Figure 28-2, a normal 15-day post-Peak phase occurred despite overall cycle prolongation to 42 days.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "This was followed by a normal 15-day, post-Peak phase but the cycle length itself was prolonged at 42 days in duration." Section: Figure 28-2.

facts.claim: In the bereavement case, recovery from the stress-induced cycle aberration took approximately two cycles before ovulation returned to normal.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "it took about two cycles for her ovulation cycles to recuperate from the stressful event." Section: Figure 28-2.

facts.claim: Pope Paul VI Institute primary luteal phase support for PMS is HCG 2000 IU intramuscularly on Peak +3, 5, 7, and 9.

source.title: Chapter 29: Premenstrual Syndrome: Evaluation and Treatment
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "human chorionic gonadotropin (HCG) given in a dosage of 2000 units Peak +3, 5, 7 and 9" Section: Treatment Protocol.

facts.claim: Oral micronized sustained-release progesterone for PMS at Pope Paul VI Institute is dosed 200 mg PO QD hs from Peak +3 through Peak +12, with option to titrate to BID.

source.title: Chapter 29: Premenstrual Syndrome: Evaluation and Treatment
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "oral micronized, sustained-release 200 mg capsule given by mouth every day at bedtime (PO QD hs), Peak +3 through P+12" Section: Treatment Protocol.

facts.claim: Vaginal progesterone for PMS at Pope Paul VI Institute uses 300 mg micronized oral capsule administered vaginally QD hs from Peak +3 through Peak +12.

source.title: Chapter 29: Premenstrual Syndrome: Evaluation and Treatment
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "300 mg micronized oral capsule is used vaginally every day at bedtime (QD hs) from Peak +3 through P+12" Section: Treatment Protocol.

facts.claim: Non-targeted progesterone therapy (starting on a fixed cycle day) creates its own endocrinopathy by administering progesterone before ovulation in cycles with later ovulation, disrupting follicular development.

source.title: Chapter 29: Premenstrual Syndrome: Evaluation and Treatment
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "a non-targeted approach to progesterone therapy creates its own endocrinopathy as a result of the exogenously administered progesterone" Section: Non-Targeted vs Targeted Progesterone Therapy.

facts.claim: Targeted luteal phase support can alternatively be delivered as intramuscular progesterone on Peak +3, 5, 7, 9, and 11.

source.title: Chapter 29: Premenstrual Syndrome: Evaluation and Treatment
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Targeted therapy can also be provided by giving intramuscular progesterone on Peak +3, 5, 7, 9 and 11" Section: Non-Targeted vs Targeted Progesterone Therapy.

facts.claim: Ovulation occurs on or before day 14 of the menstrual cycle in only 34.7 percent of cycles; ovulation occurs after day 14 in 65.3 percent of cycles, and at day 20, 7.6 percent still await ovulation.

source.title: Chapter 29: Premenstrual Syndrome: Evaluation and Treatment
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "percentage of cycles in which ovulation occurs prior to and including the 14th day of the menstrual cycle is only 34.7 percent" Section: Critical Importance of Timing.

facts.claim: The Pope Paul VI Institute PMS evaluation program requires three charted cycles before the fourth-cycle treatment implementation, providing reproducible targeted endocrine assessment.

source.title: Chapter 29: Premenstrual Syndrome: Evaluation and Treatment
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "This program, from point of entry until the cycle of treatment, requires three cycles with treatment coming in the fourth cycle" Section: Evaluation Protocol.

facts.claim: Pope Paul VI Institute PMS evaluation draws progesterone and E2 levels on Peak +3, 5, 7, 9 and 11; plus Peak +5, 7, 9 beta-endorphin; plus Peak +7 TSH, thyroid profile, total T4, reverse T3, and T3/rT3 ratio.

source.title: Chapter 29: Premenstrual Syndrome: Evaluation and Treatment
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "progesterone and E₂ level drawn on Peak +3, 5, 7, 9 and 11...Peak +5, 7 and 9 β-endorphin level is drawn, a Peak +7 TSH, thyroid profile" Section: Evaluation Protocol.

facts.claim: Hilgers published a foundational two-volume work titled 'The Scientific Foundations of the Ovulation Method' in 1995 through Pope Paul VI Institute Press, Omaha.

source.title: Chapter 3: NaProTECHNOLOGY and the New Humanism
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Mirkes R

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "T. W. Hilgers, The Scientific Foundations of the Ovulation Method (Omaha: Pope Paul VI Institute Press, 1995)" Section: References, n25.

facts.claim: Dr. Hilgers credits the prodigious research of Drs. John and Lyn Billings on the Ovulation Method, which began in 1953, as foundational to the development of NaProTECHNOLOGY.

source.title: Chapter 3: NaProTECHNOLOGY and the New Humanism
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Mirkes R

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Drs. John and Lyn Billings, whose prodigious research on the Ovulation Method began in 1953" Section: I. NPT: The Vision of Its Pioneer, Originality and Creativity.

facts.claim: Dr. Erik Odeblad's research, foundational to NaProTECHNOLOGY, focused on two foci: the biophysical characteristics of cervical mucus throughout the menstrual cycle and the mapping of the endocervix.

source.title: Chapter 3: NaProTECHNOLOGY and the New Humanism
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Mirkes R

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the biophysical characteristics of cervical mucus throughout the menstrual cycle and the mapping of the endocervix" Section: I. NPT: The Vision of Its Pioneer, Originality and Creativity.

facts.claim: Most LUFs occur in cycles with a normal-length post-Peak phase; a normal post-Peak phase does not confirm ovulation occurred.

source.title: Chapter 31: Ovarian Cysts: Evaluation and Treatment
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Most LUFs occur in cycles where the post-Peak phase is normal in length." Section: Persistent Luteal Cysts.

facts.claim: Persistent follicular cysts present on the CrMS chart as a prolonged Peak-type mucus discharge with a delayed Peak Day, reflecting unopposed estrogen dominance.

source.title: Chapter 31: Ovarian Cysts: Evaluation and Treatment
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the CrMS will reveal a prolonged Peak-type mucus discharge and a delayed Peak Day" Section: Persistent Follicular Cysts.

facts.claim: A post-Peak phase of 16 or more days on the CrMS chart is suspicious for a persistent luteal cyst (luteinized unruptured follicle, LUF).

source.title: Chapter 31: Ovarian Cysts: Evaluation and Treatment
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the post-Peak phase of the cycle may be prolonged to 16 or more days in duration." Section: Persistent Luteal Cysts.

facts.claim: In the CREIGHTON MODEL System, normal menstrual periods that follow prior progesterone production exhibit crescendo/decrescendo (L-H-M-L-VL) or decrescendo (H-H-M-M-L) bleeding patterns.

source.title: Chapter 32: Unusual Bleeding: Evaluation and Treatment
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Crescendo/Decrescendo: L-H-M-L-VL Decrescendo: H-H-M-M-L" Section: Normal Menstruation.

facts.claim: CrMS defines postmenstrual brown bleeding (tail-end brown, TEB) as two or more days of brown/black bleeding at the tail-end of menses; menses length is generally six days or longer but this is not essential.

source.title: Chapter 32: Unusual Bleeding: Evaluation and Treatment
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Two or more days of brown (black) bleeding appearing at the tail-end of the menstrual flow." Section: CREIGHTON MODEL Definitions.

facts.claim: CrMS defines premenstrual bleeding (PMB) as three or more days of light, very light, or brown bleeding occurring prior to the first moderate day of menstrual bleeding.

source.title: Chapter 32: Unusual Bleeding: Evaluation and Treatment
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Three or more days of light or very light or brown bleeding occurring prior to the beginning of the first moderate day of menstrual bleeding" Section: CREIGHTON MODEL Definitions.

facts.claim: CrMS defines excessively heavy menses as at least one 24-48 hour period where the woman must change pads, tampons or both more frequently than every two hours.

source.title: Chapter 32: Unusual Bleeding: Evaluation and Treatment
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "At least one 24 to 48 hour period where the woman must change pads, tampons or both more frequently than every two hours." Section: CREIGHTON MODEL Definitions.

facts.claim: Pope Paul VI Institute research found that in abnormal cycles, the average duration of tail-end brown bleeding was 2.2 days.

source.title: Chapter 32: Unusual Bleeding: Evaluation and Treatment
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the average duration of tail-end brown bleeding in those cycles which are considered to be abnormal, was 2.2 days" Section: CREIGHTON MODEL Definitions.

facts.claim: The Pope Paul VI Institute menstrual scoring system defines a normal menstrual flow as a mean score >6.0, with per-day values H=5, M=4, L=3, VL=2, B=1 summed over the first 6 days and divided by 6.

source.title: Chapter 32: Unusual Bleeding: Evaluation and Treatment
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.claim: In 11 cycles exhibiting very light periods, the mean menstrual score was 3.0 and the average length was 3.7 days.

source.title: Chapter 32: Unusual Bleeding: Evaluation and Treatment
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "In 11 cycles with very light periods, the average score was 3.0 and the average length in days 3.7." Section: CREIGHTON MODEL Definitions.

facts.claim: In Pope Paul VI Institute research, a decreased post-Peak estradiol-17β (E₂) sum was associated with significantly lower bone densities in the spine and the femoral neck in reproductive-age women.

source.title: Chapter 14 The role of androgens in osteoporosis and frailty
source.authors: Choong K, Bhasin S

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Patients with decreased post-Peak E₂ sums had significantly lower bone densities in the spine and the femoral neck" Section: Pope Paul VI Institute Research.

facts.claim: In reproductive-age women with CrMS mucus cycle scores ≤5.3, hip BMD was significantly lower (p=.015 total hip; p=.002) and spine BMD was near-significant (p=.055) compared to women with higher mucus cycle scores.

source.title: Chapter 14 The role of androgens in osteoporosis and frailty
source.authors: Choong K, Bhasin S

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "women who had mucus cycle scores less than or equal to 5.3, the bone mineral density for the hip was significantly lower" Section: Pope Paul VI Institute Research.

facts.claim: When the CrMS menstrual score was ≤5.1, BMD for the femoral neck (p=.041) and total hip (p=.048) were statistically lower than when the menstrual score was ≥6.0 in reproductive-age women.

source.title: Chapter 14 The role of androgens in osteoporosis and frailty
source.authors: Choong K, Bhasin S

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "When the menstrual score was less than or equal to 5.1, the BMD for the femoral neck and total hip were statistically lower" Section: Pope Paul VI Institute Research.

facts.claim: The CrMS mucus cycle score and menstrual score are hormone-dependent biomarkers; decreased mucus cycle scores are generally thought to be associated with decreased periovulatory E₂ levels.

facts.claim: In a 15-year Pope Paul VI Institute study of 28 women who developed breast cancer, 27 (96.4%) had regular cycles; only 1 (3.6%) had irregular cycles (with PCOD).
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "In this group of 28 patients, 27 (96.4%) had regular cycles. Only one (3.6%) had irregular cycles" Section: Breast Cancer.

facts.claim: Among 16 breast cancer patients, 13 (81.2%) had post-Peak phase variability of 4+ days versus only 2 of 20 controls (10.0%); statistically significant (p<.0001).
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "13 patients had a post-Peak phase with a variability of four or more days in duration (81.2%) compared to a control group of only 2 patients (10.0%)" Section: Breast Cancer.

facts.claim: CrMS biomarkers preceding breast cancer include premenstrual spotting, limited mucus cycles, and tail-end brown bleeding (TEB), all suggesting decreased progesterone production.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "premenstrual spotting, which is a biomarker of the decreased progesterone production" and "two days of tail-end brown bleeding (TEB), which suggests decreased progesterone production." Section: Breast Cancer.

facts.claim: The Treloar dataset (started 1934, 50+ years of menstrual events) showed women with extreme cycle length at ages 25-29 had nearly twofold increased breast cancer incidence.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "women who had cycles of extreme length at ages 25 to 29 years had a nearly twofold increased incidence of breast cancer." Section: Breast Cancer.

facts.claim: Adenomatous hyperplasia patients have delayed Peak Days and long mucus cycles, but overall cycles of 32 to 36 days, suggesting relative estrogen overexposure or progesterone underexposure.
facts.verification_notes: Quote: "women who have adenomatous hyperplasia also have delayed Peak Days and long mucus cycles. However, their overall cycles are 32 to 36 days in duration" Section: Endometrial Cancer.

facts.claim: Hilgers reported average luteal phase length of 13 days with a range of 9 to 17 days (86.3% of cycles) across 24 subjects over 73 menstrual cycles.

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Once ovulation does occur, the egg lives for only 12-24 hours if it is not fertilized." Section: The Egg Lives Only 12-24 Hours.

facts.claim: Without cervical mucus, sperm placed in the vagina die within hours or minutes; with good mucus, sperm may survive 3 to 5 days.

source.title: Introductory Chapter: A Global Presentation on Trends in Food Processing
source.authors: Alina Marc R

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Without mucus: Sperm will die within hours or even minutes... With good mucus: Sperm may live for 3 to 5 days" Section: Sperm Survival.

facts.claim: Estrogen reaches peak levels approximately one day before ovulation, stimulating cervical crypts to produce Type E mucus that enables sperm penetration.

source.title: Introductory Chapter: A Global Presentation on Trends in Food Processing
source.authors: Alina Marc R

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The hormone estrogen reaches a very high level or a peak approximately one day before ovulation." Section: Estrogen Peak.

facts.claim: Per Odeblad's research, Type E (estrogen) mucus arranges in parallel strands forming swimming channels for sperm; Type G (progesterone) mucus is thick and dense and blocks sperm.

source.title: Introductory Chapter: A Global Presentation on Trends in Food Processing
source.authors: Alina Marc R

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Arranges itself in parallel strands, forming swimming channels for the sperm" Section: The Biological Valve (Map of the Cervix).

facts.claim: The CrMS Peak Day is defined as the last day on which cervical mucus is clear, stretchy, or lubricative.

source.title: Introductory Chapter: A Global Presentation on Trends in Food Processing
source.authors: Alina Marc R

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Peak Day: The last day on which the mucus is clear, stretchy or lubricative" Section: The Circle Diagram.

facts.claim: Research has shown ovulation can occur up until the third day after Peak Day, but never on the fourth day after Peak; this is why CrMS counts three days post-Peak as fertile.

source.title: Introductory Chapter: A Global Presentation on Trends in Food Processing
source.authors: Alina Marc R

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "ovulation can occur up until the third day after the Peak. There has, however, never been an ovulation detected on the fourth day after the Peak." Section: Common Questions.

facts.claim: Women who chart the Creighton Model System (CrMS) and have limited mucus cycles have a significantly increased incidence of ovulation-related abnormalities, which are precursors to infertility, miscarriage, and ectopic pregnancy.

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Women who chart the CrMS and have limited mucus cycles do have a significantly increased incidence of ovulation-related abnormalities." Section: Evaluation of the Mucus Cycle.

facts.claim: The cumulus oophorus appears larger than normal in certain ovulatory defects including LUF syndrome, suggesting CO size may have diagnostic value for identifying genetically healthy cycles.

facts.claim: In the CrMS, the presence of a limited mucus cycle increases the risk of reproductive abnormalities, though some women with limited mucus cycles achieve normal pregnancies with normal outcomes.

source.title: Chapter 7: Basic Charting and Chart Reading

source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.claim: The Seminal Fluid Instruction directs the woman to urinate within one hour of intercourse, bear down with alternating Kegel exercises, and wipe until seminal fluid is gone.

source.title: Chapter 9: Basic CrMS Instructions
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

source.title: Chapter 9: Basic CrMS Instructions
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.claim: Any observation after the Seminal Fluid Instruction is never assumed to be seminal fluid but always assumed to be cervical mucus.

source.title: Chapter 9: Basic CrMS Instructions
source.journal: The Medical and Surgical Practice of NaProTECHNOLOGY
source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Whatever is observed is never assumed to be seminal fluid—it is always assumed to be cervical mucus and instructions are followed accordingly." Section: Key Principle.

source.authors: Hilgers TW

facts.claim: In 20 of 21 cycles (95.2%) with an observed LH surge, the peak day in cervical mucus fell within plus or minus 3 days of the LH surge.

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "ferning is also discovered in male saliva and can be found throughout the female cycle" Section: Discussion.

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "would recommend that the hand-held microscopes not be used as a means for avoiding pregnancy" Section: Discussion.

facts.claim: Substantial human fecundity is restricted to approximately 5-6 days of the menstrual cycle, making the timing of intercourse a strong determinant of expected pregnancies.

source.authors: Mikolajczyk RT, Stanford JB

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "actual substantial fecundity is restricted to approximately 5–6 days of the menstrual cycle" Section: Introduction.

facts.claim: The new method's reference dataset comprised 725 cycles from 125 NFP users with 6 serial non-pregnancy cycles, using the peak mucus day (last day of highly fertile vulvar discharge) as the biological marker of ovulation.

source.title: A new method for estimating the effectiveness of emergency contraception that accounts for variation in timing of ovulation and previous cycle length
source.authors: Mikolajczyk RT, Stanford JB

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "725 cycles from 125 women who had information available from 6 serial cycles without pregnancy" Section: New Method for Estimating EC Effectiveness.

facts.claim: The peak mucus day is defined as the last day of vulvar discharge with highly fertile characteristics, and serves as a biological marker of ovulation.

source.title: A new method for estimating the effectiveness of emergency contraception that accounts for variation in timing of ovulation and previous cycle length
source.authors: Mikolajczyk RT, Stanford JB

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The biomarker used was the peak mucus day, defined as the last day of vulvar discharge with highly fertile characteristics" Section: New Method.

facts.claim: The fecund window in the new method spans 14 days, from day 23 to day 10 counting backward from the last day before subsequent menses, with daily pregnancy probability peaking at day 14 (0.1215).

source.title: A new method for estimating the effectiveness of emergency contraception that accounts for variation in timing of ovulation and previous cycle length
source.authors: Mikolajczyk RT, Stanford JB

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "a 14-day fecund window of days 10 to 23, counting backward from the last day before subsequent menses" Section: New Method. Table 1 shows peak probability 0.1215 at day 14.

facts.claim: Assuming a fixed 28-day cycle for all women (idealized cycle reporting) caused all methods to substantially overestimate EC effectiveness, with the new method showing 15 points of bias at 50% true effectiveness.

source.title: A new method for estimating the effectiveness of emergency contraception that accounts for variation in timing of ovulation and previous cycle length
source.authors: Mikolajczyk RT, Stanford JB

facts.claim: Using the most recent cycle length was as accurate for predicting the fecund window as averaging the previous five cycle lengths; no advantage was found from including additional prior cycles.

facts.claim: In the HOPE study, peri-conceptional BPA exposure showed no association with time to pregnancy in women (adjusted FOR 1.07, 95% CI 0.75-1.53) after controlling for education and age.

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "FOR 1.07, 95% CI 0.75, 1.53" Section: Results.

facts.claim: Within-couple agreement of BPA exposure categories was poor in HOPE (Cohen's kappa 0.25), and within-woman reproducibility across cycles was moderate (ICC 0.52).

source.authors: Yeum D, Ju S, Cox KJ, Zhang Y, Stanford JB, Porucznik CA

facts.claim: The HOPE study enrolled 164 heterosexual couples (164 women, 163 men) with available BPA data, of whom 125 (76%) became pregnant during follow-up of up to 12 months.

facts.claim: HOPE women collected first-morning urine samples daily from the start of the fertile window through onset of menses or estimated day of ovulation +18 days; men collected daily until estimated day of ovulation.

facts.claim: In a Tongan trial, 282 women used the ovulation method for a total of 2,503 months, with one method failure and two user failures.

source.authors: Weissmann MC, Foliaki L, Billings EL, Billings JJ

source.authors: Weissmann MC, Foliaki L, Billings EL, Billings JJ

facts.claim: In this cohort, sonography modality was patient-chosen as transvaginal in 58% and transabdominal in 42% of women, with no measurable difference in ovulation detection or mean follicular size between modalities.

facts.claim: In countries where access to ART is widespread, more births occur in subfertile couples through non-ART treatment than through ART.

source.title: Is ART utilization the best indicator of access to fertility care?
source.authors: Stanford JB, James G, McLindon LA

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "there are more births in subfertile couples through non-ART treatment than through ART" Section: Letter body.

facts.claim: ART is not required for many subfertile couples to have a child, and overuse of ART may potentially be harmful.

source.title: Is ART utilization the best indicator of access to fertility care?
source.authors: Stanford JB, James G, McLindon LA

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "it is not required for many subfertile couples, and overuse of ART may potentially be harmful" Section: Letter body.

facts.claim: Limiting fertility care metrics solely to ART utilization may unnecessarily encourage overutilization of ART.

source.title: Is ART utilization the best indicator of access to fertility care?
source.authors: Stanford JB, James G, McLindon LA

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Focusing solely on ART as a metric may unnecessarily encourage overutilization" Section: Letter body.

facts.claim: The focus on ART outcomes in national and international fertility registries has stunted the scientific development of non-ART treatments, including those addressing underlying health conditions.

source.title: Is ART utilization the best indicator of access to fertility care?
source.authors: Stanford JB, James G, McLindon LA

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "focus on ART... has stunted the scientific development of non-ART treatments" Section: Letter body.

facts.claim: The ultimate measure of access to fertility care should be the proportion of those desiring fertility who achieve a healthy live birth, whether or not ART is required.

facts.claim: Registry assessments should be developed and supported for all fertility treatments, not only ART, to improve outcomes and promote robust consumer choice.

facts.claim: In 90 nondiabetic subjects evaluated with both the pancreatic suppression test (PST) and OGTT, 37 (41.1%) were classified as insulin resistant and 53 (58.9%) as non-insulin-resistant.

source.title: A Simple and Improved Predictor of Insulin Resistance Extracted From the Oral  Glucose Tolerance Test: The I0*G60
source.authors: Contreras PH, Salgado AM, Bernal YA, Vigil P

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "37 (41.1%), were categorized as insulin resistant (IR subjects) by the PST, and the remaining 53 patients (58.9%)" Section: Materials and Methods.

facts.claim: The pancreatic suppression test (PST) has a correlation of 0.93 with hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp results, making it a clinically applicable alternative to the gold-standard clamp.

source.title: A Simple and Improved Predictor of Insulin Resistance Extracted From the Oral  Glucose Tolerance Test: The I0*G60
source.authors: Contreras PH, Salgado AM, Bernal YA, Vigil P

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Greenfield et al. [9] claimed that it has a correlation of 0.93 with the clamp results" Section: Introduction.

facts.claim: At fasting, mean insulin in IR subjects was 15.8 ± 1.8 mIU/mL versus 6.3 ± 0.5 mIU/mL in NIR subjects (P<0.0001); at 90 minutes, IR subjects peaked at 149.4 mIU/mL versus 48.6 in NIR.

source.title: A Simple and Improved Predictor of Insulin Resistance Extracted From the Oral  Glucose Tolerance Test: The I0*G60
source.authors: Contreras PH, Salgado AM, Bernal YA, Vigil P

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "NIR, n 5 53 6.3 6 0.5... IR, n 5 37 15.8 6 1.8" Section: Table 2 OGTT values.

facts.claim: The I0*G60 outperformed the Matsuda-DeFronzo ISI composite (the best traditional predictor) on AUROC (0.867 vs 0.835), Youden index (0.657 vs 0.585), sensitivity (0.865 vs 0.811), and global accuracy (0.822 vs 0.789).

source.title: A Simple and Improved Predictor of Insulin Resistance Extracted From the Oral  Glucose Tolerance Test: The I0*G60
source.authors: Contreras PH, Salgado AM, Bernal YA, Vigil P

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "clearly surpassed that of the ISI composite... in AUROC (0.867 vs 0.835), Youden index (0.657 vs 0.585), sensitivity (0.865 vs 0.811)" Section: Results.

facts.claim: The I0*G60 predictor (basal insulin times glucose at 60 minutes) had a correlation coefficient of 0.697 with steady-state plasma glucose (SSPG) and an AUROC of 0.867 for diagnosing insulin resistance.

source.title: A Simple and Improved Predictor of Insulin Resistance Extracted From the Oral  Glucose Tolerance Test: The I0*G60
source.authors: Contreras PH, Salgado AM, Bernal YA, Vigil P

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the I0*G60 had a correlation coefficient of 0.697 with the SSPG and an AUROC of 0.867" Section: Abstract/Results.

facts.claim: The I0*G60 cutoff predicting insulin resistance was >1110 mg/dL*mIU/mL (>428 nM*pM), with sensitivity 0.865 and global accuracy 0.822.

source.title: A Simple and Improved Predictor of Insulin Resistance Extracted From the Oral  Glucose Tolerance Test: The I0*G60
source.authors: Contreras PH, Salgado AM, Bernal YA, Vigil P

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Its cutoff predicting IR was .1110 mg/dL*mIU/mL (.428 nM*pM), its sensitivity was 0.865, and its global accuracy was 0.822" Section: Abstract/Results.

facts.claim: The pancreatic suppression test (PST) infuses glucose at 267 mg/m^2/min, crystalline insulin at 32 mU/m^2/min, and octreotide at 0.27 mcg/m^2/min for 3 hours, with SSPG averaged from glucose values at 150-180 minutes.

source.title: A Simple and Improved Predictor of Insulin Resistance Extracted From the Oral  Glucose Tolerance Test: The I0*G60
source.authors: Contreras PH, Salgado AM, Bernal YA, Vigil P

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "continuous infusion of glucose (267 mg/m 2 /min), crystalline insulin (32 mU/m 2 /min), and octreotide (0.27 mg/m2 /min" Section: Pancreatic Suppression Test.

facts.claim: An SSPG value <150 mg/dL indicates non-insulin-resistant status, while SSPG >=150 mg/dL indicates insulin resistance.

source.title: A Simple and Improved Predictor of Insulin Resistance Extracted From the Oral  Glucose Tolerance Test: The I0*G60
source.authors: Contreras PH, Salgado AM, Bernal YA, Vigil P

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "SSPG values ,150 mg/dL indicate an NIR condition; values $150 mg/dL indicate an IR condition" Section: Pancreatic Suppression Test.

facts.claim: Among insulin-resistant subjects, mean SSPG was 229.4 ± 9.3 mg/dL (range 151-375) versus 90.8 ± 4.2 mg/dL (range 39-149) in non-insulin-resistant subjects (P<0.0001).

source.title: A Simple and Improved Predictor of Insulin Resistance Extracted From the Oral  Glucose Tolerance Test: The I0*G60
source.authors: Contreras PH, Salgado AM, Bernal YA, Vigil P

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "SSPG of the NIR subjects was 90.8 6 4.2 mg/dL (39 to 149 mg/dL), whereas the corresponding value of the IR subjects was 229.4 6 9.3 mg/dL" Section: Results.

facts.claim: Basal insulin (I0) had the highest AUROC (0.822) among individual OGTT values for predicting insulin resistance, but its sensitivity was only 0.568, limiting clinical utility.

source.title: A Simple and Improved Predictor of Insulin Resistance Extracted From the Oral  Glucose Tolerance Test: The I0*G60
source.authors: Contreras PH, Salgado AM, Bernal YA, Vigil P

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "basal insulin value, despite its high AUROC, displayed a low sensitivity (0.568), thus limiting its predictive power" Section: Results.

facts.claim: 43.2% of insulin-resistant subjects had basal insulin (I0) values below 13.2 mIU/mL, meaning fasting insulin alone misses nearly half of IR cases.

source.title: A Simple and Improved Predictor of Insulin Resistance Extracted From the Oral  Glucose Tolerance Test: The I0*G60
source.authors: Contreras PH, Salgado AM, Bernal YA, Vigil P

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "43.2% of our IR subjects had an I0 ,13.2 mIU/mL" Section: Discussion.

source.authors: Porucznik CA, Cox KJ, Stanford JB, Schliep KC

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the Peak Day "best quality" algorithm, based upon cervical fluid, identified ovulation ± 3 days of the urine monitor in 24 cycles (92%)" Section: Abstract/Results.

facts.claim: Among 40 women wishing to conceive in the study, 31 (78%) achieved pregnancy within 4 cycles using the Peak Day method.

source.title: Pilot test and validation of the peak day method of prospective determination of ovulation against a handheld urine hormone monitor
source.authors: Porucznik CA, Cox KJ, Stanford JB, Schliep KC

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "of those wishing to get pregnant, most conceived within 4 cycles (31/40 = 78%)" Section: Results.

facts.claim: In a comparator study without cervical mucus monitoring, only 56% of women estimated the day of ovulation within ±2 days of the CBFM, versus 88% with the Peak Day 'best quality' algorithm.

source.title: Pilot test and validation of the peak day method of prospective determination of ovulation against a handheld urine hormone monitor
source.authors: Porucznik CA, Cox KJ, Stanford JB, Schliep KC

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "only 56% of women estimated the day of ovulation within ±2 days of the CBFM identified peak day" Section: Discussion.

facts.claim: Of 147 cycles evaluated, women selected an estimated day of ovulation in 130 cycles (88%) and completed the periovulatory exposure questionnaire in 122 (94%) of cycles where an EDO was identified.

source.title: Pilot test and validation of the peak day method of prospective determination of ovulation against a handheld urine hormone monitor
source.authors: Porucznik CA, Cox KJ, Stanford JB, Schliep KC

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "women selected an EDO in 130 (88%) and subsequently completed the periovulatory exposure questionnaire in 122 (94%) cycles" Section: Abstract/Results.

facts.claim: The 'best quality' algorithm identifies the last day of a cycle when a woman observed the most number of three fertile-type cervical fluid characteristics: slippery, stretchy, and/or clear.

source.title: Pilot test and validation of the peak day method of prospective determination of ovulation against a handheld urine hormone monitor
source.authors: Porucznik CA, Cox KJ, Stanford JB, Schliep KC

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "identifies the last day during a menstrual cycle when a woman observed the most number of the three fertile-type cervical fluid characteristics" Section: Fertility chart expert review.

facts.claim: Compared to the urine monitor, the 'best quality' algorithm matched within ±1 day in 65% of cycles, ±2 days in 88%, and ±3 days in 92% of 26 validation cycles.

source.authors: Porucznik CA, Cox KJ, Stanford JB, Schliep KC

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "± 1 day of the monitor in 65% of cycles, ± 2 days of the monitor in 88% of cycles, and ± 3 days of the monitor in 92% of cycles" Section: Results.

facts.claim: The 'last fertile sign' algorithm matched the CBFM exactly in 19% of cycles and within ±2/±3 days in 77% of cycles when excluding seven days prior to menses.

facts.claim: In China, an estimated 63% of men and 3.8% of women are smokers, creating high background rates of environmental tobacco smoke (ETS) exposure for women.

source.title: Effect of environmental tobacco smoke on levels of urinary hormone markers
source.authors: Chen C, Wang X, Wang L, Yang F, Tang G, Xing H, Ryan L, Lasley B, Overstreet JW, Stanford JB, Xu X

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "In China, for example, 63% of men and only 3.8% of women are estimated to be smokers" Section: Introduction.

facts.claim: Of 673 analyzed cycles, 597 had ETS exposure and only 76 had no ETS exposure, reflecting near-universal passive smoke exposure in this nonsmoking cohort due to husbands' smoking.

source.title: Effect of environmental tobacco smoke on levels of urinary hormone markers
source.authors: Chen C, Wang X, Wang L, Yang F, Tang G, Xing H, Ryan L, Lasley B, Overstreet JW, Stanford JB, Xu X

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "76 cycles did not have ETS exposure and 597 cycles had ETS exposure" Section: Results.

facts.claim: Conception was defined as a Bayesian-modeled probability ≥ 0.9 based on daily urinary hCG values, using control cycles from non-conception women and clinical pregnancy cycles to fit the model.

source.title: Effect of environmental tobacco smoke on levels of urinary hormone markers
source.authors: Chen C, Wang X, Wang L, Yang F, Tang G, Xing H, Ryan L, Lasley B, Overstreet JW, Stanford JB, Xu X

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "We defined conception as a probability ≥0.9." Section: Conception status.

facts.claim: The authors conclude that the adverse reproductive effects of environmental tobacco smoke may act in part through antiestrogen mechanisms, given the significantly decreased E1C levels observed in nonconception cycles.

source.title: Effect of environmental tobacco smoke on levels of urinary hormone markers
source.authors: Chen C, Wang X, Wang L, Yang F, Tang G, Xing H, Ryan L, Lasley B, Overstreet JW, Stanford JB, Xu X

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "adverse reproductive effect of ETS may act in part through its antiestrogen effects" Section: Discussion/Summary.

facts.claim: In nonconception cycles, ETS exposure was associated with significantly lower urinary estrone conjugate (E1C) levels (β = -0.43, SE = 0.08, p < 0.001 in log scale) compared with non-exposed cycles.

source.title: Effect of environmental tobacco smoke on levels of urinary hormone markers
source.authors: Chen C, Wang X, Wang L, Yang F, Tang G, Xing H, Ryan L, Lasley B, Overstreet JW, Stanford JB, Xu X

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "ETS exposure was found to be associated with a lower urinary E1C levels (β= –0.43, SE = 0.08, p < 0.001 in log scale)" Section: Results.

facts.claim: In conception cycles, the association between ETS exposure and urinary E1C was not statistically significant (β = -0.17, SE = 0.10, p = 0.085 in log scale).

source.title: Effect of environmental tobacco smoke on levels of urinary hormone markers
source.authors: Chen C, Wang X, Wang L, Yang F, Tang G, Xing H, Ryan L, Lasley B, Overstreet JW, Stanford JB, Xu X

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Among conception cycles, the association was not significant (β= –0.17, SE = 0.10, p = 0.085 in log scale)" Section: Results.

facts.claim: ETS exposure showed no significant difference in urinary pregnanediol-3-glucuronide (PdG, the main progesterone metabolite) levels regardless of conception status.

source.title: Effect of environmental tobacco smoke on levels of urinary hormone markers
source.authors: Chen C, Wang X, Wang L, Yang F, Tang G, Xing H, Ryan L, Lasley B, Overstreet JW, Stanford JB, Xu X

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "There was no significant difference in PdG level between nonexposed and exposed women regardless of conception status." Section: Results.

facts.claim: The median time to clinical pregnancy among newly married, nulliparous Chinese textile workers stopping contraception was 3 cycles.

source.title: Effect of environmental tobacco smoke on levels of urinary hormone markers
source.authors: Chen C, Wang X, Wang L, Yang F, Tang G, Xing H, Ryan L, Lasley B, Overstreet JW, Stanford JB, Xu X

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The median time to clinical pregnancy was three cycles." Section: Results.

facts.claim: Day of ovulation was determined by two independent methods: a published E1C:PdG ratio algorithm (Baird 1995) and a two-piecewise regression model identifying the PdG rising point; only cycles where both methods agreed within ±3 days were included.

source.title: Effect of environmental tobacco smoke on levels of urinary hormone markers

facts.claim: In this cohort, the average follicular phase length was 15.8 days (range 6–39) and the average luteal phase was 15.0 days (range 6–33).

source.title: Effect of environmental tobacco smoke on levels of urinary hormone markers

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "average length of follicular phase was 15.8 days with a range of 6 to 39 days and luteal phase was 15.0 days with a range of 6 to 33 days" Section: ETS exposure and urinary hormone profiles.

facts.claim: Urinary PdG and E1C were measured by enzyme immunoassay with minimum detection levels of 3 ng/mL (PdG) and 0.096 ng/mL (E1C); coefficients of variation were 4.3% and 5.1%, respectively.

source.title: Effect of environmental tobacco smoke on levels of urinary hormone markers

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "minimum detection levels for PdG and E1C were 3 ng/mL and 0.096 ng/mL, respectively, and the coefficients of variation" Section: Laboratory assays.

facts.claim: The PrePARED consortium combines 23 cohort studies with data on nearly 200,000 women to investigate effects of preconception exposures on perinatal outcomes.

source.title: The Preconception Period analysis of Risks and Exposures Influencing health and Development (PrePARED) consortium

source.title: The Preconception Period analysis of Risks and Exposures Influencing health and Development (PrePARED) consortium
source.authors: Harville EW, Mishra GD, Yeung E, Mumford SL, Schisterman EF, Jukic AM, Hatch EE, Mikkelsen EM, Jiang H, Ehrenthal DB, Porucznik CA, Wen SW, Harvey A, Symons Downs D, Yajnik C, Santillan D, Santillan M, McElrath TF, Woo JG, Urbina EM, Chavarro JE, Sotres-Alvarez D, Bazzano L, Zhang J, Steiner A, Gunderson EP, Wise LA, Stanford JB

facts.claim: The PrePARED consortium investigates preconception exposures across four outcome categories: fertility/miscarriage, pregnancy-related conditions, perinatal/child health, and adult health outcomes.

source.title: The Preconception Period analysis of Risks and Exposures Influencing health and Development (PrePARED) consortium

facts.claim: The term 'rhythm method' derives its name from Leo Latz's 1932 book 'The Rhythm of Sterility and Fertility in Women,' which described a calendar-based method of natural family planning.

source.title: The Linacre Quarterly, Rhythm, NFP, and Human Fertility from Popes Pius XI  through Benedict XVI
source.authors: Fehring RJ, Yeung PP Jr

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "a very simple calendar-based method of natural family planning that today takes the name 'rhythm' from his book." Section: history of NFP.

facts.claim: Bertotti's analysis of the 2006-2010 National Survey of Family Growth examined differences between current and past NFP users among reproductive-age women in the United States.

source.title: The Linacre Quarterly, Rhythm, NFP, and Human Fertility from Popes Pius XI  through Benedict XVI
source.authors: Fehring RJ, Yeung PP Jr

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Dr. Bertotti's paper analyzes data from the 2006–2010 National Survey of Family Growth to show differences in current and past users of NFP" Section: special issue contents.

facts.claim: The fertile window spans approximately 6 days, beginning about 5 days before ovulation and extending through the day of ovulation, with no documented conception on the day after ovulation.

source.title: Timing intercourse to achieve pregnancy: current evidence
source.authors: Stanford JB, White GL, Hatasaka H

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "Conception is possible from intercourse beginning about 5 days before ovulation extending through the day of ovulation." Section: The Fertile Window.

facts.claim: In a Billings Ovulation Method study, couples of normal fertility who had intercourse on the peak day had a 67% probability of pregnancy, and intercourse up to 3 days before peak day with favorable discharge yielded a 55% probability.

source.title: Timing intercourse to achieve pregnancy: current evidence
source.authors: Stanford JB, White GL, Hatasaka H

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "couples of normal fertility who have intercourse on the peak day have a 67% probability of pregnancy" Section: Fertility Charting of Vaginal Discharge.

facts.claim: Spermatozoa can survive for up to 6 days in properly estrogenized cervical mucus.

source.title: Timing intercourse to achieve pregnancy: current evidence
source.authors: Stanford JB, White GL, Hatasaka H

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "spermatozoa can survive for up to 6 days in properly estrogenized cervical mucus." Section: The Fertile Window.

facts.claim: In women with normal fertility, only 30% had their entire fertile window completely within cycle days 10-17, and on each day between cycle days 6 and 21 at least 10% of women with regular cycles were within their fertile window.

source.title: Timing intercourse to achieve pregnancy: current evidence
source.authors: Stanford JB, White GL, Hatasaka H

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "only 30% had their entire fertile window (as determined by urinary hormones) completely within days 10 and 17 of the menstrual cycle" Section: Calendar Calculations.

facts.claim: Approximately 3% of women with regular cycles were still within their fertile window on the day of expected menses by calendar calculation, making calendar methods unreliable for timing intercourse to conceive.

source.title: Timing intercourse to achieve pregnancy: current evidence
source.authors: Stanford JB, White GL, Hatasaka H

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "on the day of expected menses (by calendar calculation), about 3% of women with regular cycles were still in their fertile window." Section: Calendar Calculations.

facts.claim: The peak day identified by women using fertility charting correlates with the timing of ovulation within 2 or 3 days in all cases, as measured by serum hormones, urinary hormones, and follicular ultrasound.

source.title: Timing intercourse to achieve pregnancy: current evidence
source.authors: Stanford JB, White GL, Hatasaka H

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The peak day as identified by women correlates well with the timing of ovulation (2 or 3 days in all cases)" Section: Fertility Charting of Vaginal Discharge.

facts.claim: A US trial of the ovulation method demonstrated a method-related pregnancy rate of only 0.4%.

source.title: Ovulation method of contraception
source.authors: Billings JJ

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "A recent trial in the United States of America showed a method-related pregnancy rate of only 0.4%." Section: Billings letter response.

facts.claim: Billings argues the ovulation method is based on facts of human reproductive biology and is therefore not susceptible of improvement; the authentic method excludes the concurrent use of barrier contraception for biological reasons.

source.title: Ovulation method of contraception
source.authors: Billings JJ

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "the authentic method, for biological reasons, excludes the concurrent use of barrier contraception." Section: Billings letter conclusion.

facts.claim: A core rule of the Billings ovulation method is that coitus must be avoided during menstruation when pregnancy avoidance is the goal in the preovulatory phase, because menstrual flow can conceal cervical mucus secretion that warns of approaching ovulation.

source.title: Ovulation method of contraception
source.authors: Billings JJ

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "coitus be avoided during menstruation, if pregnancy is to be avoided in the preovulatory phase of the cycle" Section: Billings letter.

facts.claim: In the preovulatory phase, coitus under the ovulation method should occur only in the evening of a day when it has been verified that the ovulatory pattern of mucus has not begun.

source.title: Ovulation method of contraception
source.authors: Billings JJ

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "coitus should occur only in the evening of a day when it has been verified that the ovulatory pattern of mucus has not begun." Section: Billings letter.

facts.claim: Cycles as short as 20 or 21 days have been observed in which ovulation was verified by ovarian hormone measurement and which had a luteal phase of normal length, refuting the notion that ovulation can be reliably predicted by counting days from menses.

source.title: Ovulation method of contraception
source.authors: Billings JJ

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "a number of cycles of only 20 or 21 days in which ovulation was verified by measurement of the ovarian hormones" Section: Billings letter.

facts.claim: The ovulation method does not require prolonged abstinence when a persistent discharge results from prior contraceptive medication use; the basic infertile pattern (dry days or unchanging mucus) allows identification of infertility even with vaginal discharge.

source.title: Ovulation method of contraception
source.authors: Billings JJ

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "The concept of a basic infertile pattern of either dry days or unchanging mucus is both scientific and practical" Section: Billings letter.

facts.claim: Only ovulatory mucus at spinnbarkeit has the characteristics that allow passage of sperm along the cervical canal; preovulatory mucus is sperm-supporting but does not have these transport properties.

source.title: Ovulation method of contraception
source.authors: Billings JJ

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "only ovulatory mucus at spinnbarkeit has the characteristics which allow passage of sperm along the cervical canal." Section: Cattanach reply.

facts.claim: In Cattanach's series of 214 women, 81.9% had a shortest intermenstrual interval of 28-30 days, 10.3% over 30 days, 3.3% at 25-27 days, 1.9% at 22-24 days, and 2.8% at 21 days.

source.title: Ovulation method of contraception
source.authors: Billings JJ

facts.claim: In Cattanach's questionnaire data (124 valid replies), 88.7% of women completed heavy menstrual flow within three days and 95.2% within four days, with mean 5.7 pads/day (S.E. 0.44).

source.title: Ovulation method of contraception
source.authors: Billings JJ

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "88.7% had completed heavy menstrual flow...within three days and 95.2% had completed this flow within four days (mean 5.7 pads/day with S.E. 0.44)" Section: Cattanach reply.

facts.claim: In Cattanach's questionnaire data, 78.9% of women completed their menses by six days and 98.4% by seven days, with mean 2.9 pads/day (S.E. 0.17).

source.title: Ovulation method of contraception
source.authors: Billings JJ

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "78.9% had completed their menses...by six days and 98.4% by 7 days (mean: 2.9 pads/day with S.E. 0.17)." Section: Cattanach reply.

facts.claim: Obesity (BMI >= 30) affects approximately 42% of adults in the United States, with women showing a higher prevalence of severe obesity (BMI >= 40) than men (11.5% vs 6.9%) during 2017-2018.

source.title: The importance of estradiol for body weight regulation in women
source.authors: Vigil P, Meléndez J, Petkovic G, Del Río JP

facts.verification_notes: Quote: "women had a higher prevalence of severe obesity (BMI ≥ 40 kg/m 2 ) than men (11.5% vs. 6.9%, respectively)" Section: Introduction.

facts.claim: Progesterone-only contraceptives disrupt normal ovulation and decrease estradiol levels, which may decrease insulin sensitivity and alter food intake and body weight in some women.

source.title: The importance of estradiol for body weight regulation in women
source.authors: Vigil P, Meléndez J, Petkovic G, Del Río JP
